# catalystcenter_planned_access_point_position (Resource)

This resource manages a planned (virtual) Access Point position on a floor map for RF planning in Cisco Catalyst Center. Planned APs are Day 0 design-time resources - no physical APs are needed in the inventory.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "catalystcenter_planned_access_point_position" "example" {
  floor_id   = "5e6f7b3a-2b0b-4a7d-8b1c-0d4b1cd5e1b1"
  name       = "AP-1001"
  ap_type    = "AP9166I"
  position_x = 50.5
  position_y = 30.2
  position_z = 3.5
  radios = [
    {
      bands             = ["5"]
      channel           = 36
      tx_power          = 17
      antenna_name      = "Internal-CW9166I-x-5GHz"
      antenna_azimuth   = 0
      antenna_elevation = 0
    }
  ]
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `ap_type` (String) AP model type. Use /dna/intent/api/v1/maps/supported-access-points to find supported models
- `floor_id` (String) Floor ID where the planned AP is placed
- `name` (String) Name of the planned Access Point
- `position_x` (Number) X coordinate of the planned AP position in feet
- `position_y` (Number) Y coordinate of the planned AP position in feet
- `radios` (Attributes Set) Radio configurations for the planned AP (1-4 radios). Any change to any radio attribute forces destroy+create because the Catalyst Center bulkChange API has limited update support. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--radios))

### Optional

- `mac_address` (String) MAC address of the planned Access Point
- `position_z` (Number) Z coordinate (height) of the planned AP position in feet

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) The id of the object

<a id="nestedatt--radios"></a>
### Nested Schema for `radios`

Optional:

- `antenna_azimuth` (Number) Antenna azimuth angle in degrees (0-360)
- `antenna_elevation` (Number) Antenna elevation angle in degrees (-90 to 90)
- `antenna_name` (String) Antenna type name. Use /dna/intent/api/v1/maps/supported-access-points to find supported antennas
- `bands` (Set of String) Radio frequency bands in GHz. Valid values are 2.4, 5, and 6
- `channel` (Number) Channel number for map visualization
- `radio_id` (String) Radio ID (read-only, assigned by Catalyst Center)
- `tx_power` (Number) Transmit power in dBm for map visualization

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
terraform import catalystcenter_planned_access_point_position.example "<floor_id>,<name>"
```
